### Reconciliation Verdicts (19 entries)

Per D-05 closed verdict taxonomy `{CONFIRMED_FRESH_MATCHES_237, NEW_SINCE_237, SUPERSEDED_IN_237, CANDIDATE_FINDING}`. No hedged verdicts. One structured sub-entry per Row ID (19 entries).

### GO-240-001 (INV-237-052) — _gameOverEntropy (short-circuit)
**Fresh-eyes observation at HEAD 7ab515fe:** `_gameOverEntropy` body at `AdvanceModule.sol:1213-1299` contains early-return `if (rngWordByDay[day] != 0) return rngWordByDay[day];` at `:1219`; file:line matches Phase 237 Consumer Index row INV-237-052 exactly.
**Reconciliation Verdict:** CONFIRMED_FRESH_MATCHES_237
**Evidence:** `grep -n 'rngWordByDay\[day\] != 0' contracts/modules/DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol` → `1219: if (rngWordByDay[day] != 0) return rngWordByDay[day];`

**Reconciliation Verdict:** CONFIRMED_FRESH_MATCHES_237

### Per-Actor Proof Sketches (fresh re-derivation at HEAD 7ab515fe)

The 19 GO-02 actor-cell verdicts above are underwritten by the following first-principles re-derivations at HEAD. Each restates the closed-form argument independently of Phase 238/239 (which are corroborating cross-cites per D-17).

**SAFE rows (GO-240-001..007, 7 rows — `gameover-entropy` family):** The `rngLocked` gate closes every player/admin/validator influence path on `rngWordByDay[day]` between VRF request and consumption.

- **Player closure:** No permissionless function can mutate `rngWordByDay[day]`, `rngWordCurrent`, `vrfRequestId`, or `rngRequestTime` while `rngLockedFlag == true` — the revert `if (rngLockedFlag) revert RngLocked()` at `DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.sol:1031` (canonical check in `requestLootboxRng`) + the 62-row permissionless sweep in `audit/v30-PERMISSIONLESS-SWEEP.md` (0 `CANDIDATE_FINDING` rows) exhaustively closes player reach. `rngLockedFlag = true` is set atomically at `:1579` inside `_finalizeRngRequest` alongside `rngWordCurrent = 0` / `rngRequestTime = block.timestamp`; the flag clears at `:1676` (`_unlockRng`) after ticket-drain completes + `rngWordByDay[day]` is written at `:1690-1702` (`rawFulfillRandomWords` daily branch SSTORE). `rngLocked` window fully brackets the request-to-consumption interval per `audit/v30-RNGLOCK-STATE-MACHINE.md` Path `RNGLOCK-239-P-007`.
- **Admin closure:** The only admin-gated clear of `rngLockedFlag` is `updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub` at `:1635` (governance-gated via `ContractAddresses.ADMIN` check at `:1627` + sDGNRS-holder vote per KNOWN-ISSUES not re-litigated here). This clear resets `rngWordCurrent = 0` and `vrfRequestId = 0` (`:1636-1638`), so no admin action can replace an in-flight VRF word without burning the request; the next `_finalizeRngRequest` re-seeds all four state variables atomically. Admin cannot substitute a chosen word into `rngWordByDay[day]`; only `rawFulfillRandomWords` (VRF-coordinator-only; gated by `msg.sender != address(vrfCoordinator)` revert at `:1694`) writes that slot.
- **Validator closure (VRF-available branch):** VRF oracle withholds fulfillment → path routes to prevrandao fallback via `GAMEOVER_RNG_FALLBACK_DELAY` at `:109` + `:1250` — that routing produces the 8 `EXCEPTION (KI: EXC-02)` rows, not a SAFE-row exception. Validator block-delay attacks on the VRF-available branch itself cannot advance `block.timestamp` arbitrarily; any re-ordering within a valid block window has no influence on `rngWordByDay[day]` because the SSTORE is deterministic-in-`randomWords[0]` (see `:1697-1702`). Structural equivalence with Phase 238-03 GATING Heatmap §"gameover-entropy (7 rows) | 7 rngLocked | — | — | — | — | 7" row affirms this re-derivation.

**EXC-02 rows (GO-240-008..015, 8 rows — `prevrandao-fallback` family):** The 14-day `GAMEOVER_RNG_FALLBACK_DELAY` `semantic-path-gate` fully closes player/admin reach; validator retains a KI-accepted 1-bit manipulation handle on binary outcomes (the canonical KI EXC-02 exposure).

- **Player closure:** Entering `_getHistoricalRngFallback` requires `elapsed >= GAMEOVER_RNG_FALLBACK_DELAY` (14 days) at `:1250`; `elapsed = ts - rngRequestTime` where `ts = block.timestamp` and `rngRequestTime` was set atomically at `:1578` during `_finalizeRngRequest`. No player can advance `block.timestamp` by 14 days (outside single-tx sandwich-attack scope).
- **Admin closure:** Admin's only interaction with the fallback branch is the governance-gated coordinator rotation at `:1622-1651`; rotation resets `rngRequestTime = 0` at `:1637`, zeroing the 14-day elapsed counter — it cannot *advance* the counter to trigger fallback earlier.
- **Validator column EXCEPTION (KI: EXC-02):** Once the 14-day gate opens, `_getHistoricalRngFallback` at `:1301-1325` produces `keccak256(abi.encode(combined, currentDay, block.prevrandao))` at `:1322`. `block.prevrandao` is validator-proposable with 1-bit entropy per block per EIP-4399 (post-merge). On the 8-consumer prevrandao fallback cascade, this manifests as 1-bit manipulation on binary outcomes. Acceptance of this exposure as KI EXC-02 is Phase 241's scope per D-19/D-22 strict boundary. **See Phase 241 EXC-02.**

**EXC-03 rows (GO-240-016..019, 4 rows — `F-29-04` mid-cycle substitution family):** Terminal-state gameover `semantic-path-gate` + single-caller call-chain rooting closes player/admin/validator reach on jackpot-input determinism; the EXCEPTION verdict captures the substitution surface itself, not a determinism violation on the VRF-available branch.

- **Player closure:** The 4 F-29-04 rows touch write-buffer-swap + `_gameOverEntropy` VRF-available-substitution block. `advanceGame:292` `_swapAndFreeze` is gated by `advanceGame`'s external entry + `_dayIndex` transition logic (not permissionless state-write); `requestLootboxRng:1082` `_swapTicketSlot` runs *inside* `requestLootboxRng` which requires `!rngLockedFlag` at `:1031` and uses the pre-swap `purchaseLevel_`. Players cannot force a swap-to-substitution alignment without advancing the game calendar (`_endPhase`-rooted phase-transition-gate per Phase 239-03 § Asymmetry B). `_gameOverEntropy:1221-1246` consumption is reached only when `rngWordByDay[day] == 0` AND `_handleGameOverPath` returns STAGE_GAMEOVER (pool-deficit or 120-day liveness) — terminal state by construction.
- **Admin closure:** Admin has no direct toggle on `_swapAndFreeze` / `_swapTicketSlot` / `_gameOverEntropy` body; admin's `updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub` rotation resets RNG state but does not inject write-buffer substitutions.
- **Validator closure (VRF-available branch):** On the VRF-available branch, validator withholding routes to EXC-02 prevrandao (out-of-scope here); validator block-reordering cannot split a tx across blocks, so the terminal-state + write-buffer-swap atomicity holds per Phase 239-03 § Asymmetry B single-threaded-EVM argument. The EXCEPTION verdict reflects the F-29-04 substitution surface itself (mid-cycle write-buffer tickets drain under gameover VRF word instead of originally-anticipated mid-day/daily word) — Phase 241 EXC-03 owns acceptance re-verification. **See Phase 241 EXC-03.**

### Non-Player Actor Narrative

Per D-11 player-centric attacker model, GO-04's primary analytic unit is player-reachable vectors (enumerated in the GO-04 Trigger Surface Table above). ROADMAP Success Criterion 4 names "an attacker" (singular threat model); REQUIREMENTS.md GO-04 wording covers "any actor". This section delivers CLOSED VERDICTS per non-player actor (admin / validator / VRF-oracle) per D-11/D-12/D-13 with bold-labeled grep-anchored tokens for reviewer extraction. Absence of any of the 3 closed verdicts below = re-open plan per D-13 mandatory attestation.

### Admin actor

Fresh grep at HEAD `7ab515fe` over admin-gated modifier surfaces (`onlyAdmin`, `onlyOwner`, `msg.sender != ContractAddresses.ADMIN`) returns the following functions that touch gameover-adjacent state:

- `DegenerusGameAdvanceModule._updateKeyHashOnly` @ AdvanceModule:500 (`msg.sender != ContractAddresses.ADMIN`) — updates `vrfKeyHash` ONLY; no touch of `phaseTransitionActive` / `gameOver` / `rngLockedFlag` / liveness-trigger state.
- `DegenerusGameAdvanceModule.updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub` @ AdvanceModule:1627 (`msg.sender != ContractAddresses.ADMIN`) — emergency VRF coordinator rotation; RESETS rngLockedFlag = false @:1635 + vrfRequestId = 0 @:1636 + rngRequestTime = 0 @:1637 + rngWordCurrent = 0 @:1638; does NOT SSTORE to `phaseTransitionActive`, `gameOver`, `purchaseStartDay`, `dailyIdx`, or `_simulatedDayIndex` inputs (all GOTRIG-240-001 neutralizers remain intact post-rotation).
- `DegenerusAdmin.swapGameEthForStEth` @ DegenerusAdmin:631 (`onlyOwner`) — ETH↔stETH swap for gas treasury; no gameover-state touch.
- `DegenerusDeityPass` `onlyOwner` functions @:94, :108 — renderer/metadata updates; no gameover-state touch.
- No function at HEAD directly SSTOREs `phaseTransitionActive = true` outside `_endPhase:634` (single caller `advanceGame:460` per Phase 239-03 § Asymmetry B re-verified at HEAD 7ab515fe).
- No function at HEAD directly SSTOREs `gameOver = true` outside `GameOverModule.handleGameOverDrain:136` (caller: `_handleGameOverPath:620` via delegatecall, guarded by `_livenessTriggered():530`).
- No function at HEAD directly writes `purchaseStartDay` outside `advanceGame`-origin phase-transition logic (GOVAR-240-011 Named Gate `phase-transition-gate`).

**Admin closed verdict: NO_DIRECT_TRIGGER_SURFACE** — fresh-eyes grep at HEAD `7ab515fe` over all admin-gated entry points returns zero functions that (a) directly SSTORE to `phaseTransitionActive` or `gameOver` or (b) bypass the `_handleGameOverPath:530` `_livenessTriggered()` liveness gate or (c) mutate any of the GOTRIG-240-001 neutralizer sites (purchaseStartDay, _simulatedDayIndex inputs, `_livenessTriggered()` callsites). Admin's VRF coordinator rotation via `updateVrfCoordinatorAndSub:1627` resets VRF state to a FRESH pre-commitment condition (VRF state zeroed out) but cannot force a gameover trigger; rotation cannot advance `currentDay - psd` beyond `120` or advance `block.timestamp`. Cross-cites `audit/ACCESS-CONTROL-MATRIX.md` (structural admin surface scaffolding; re-verified at HEAD 7ab515fe — access-control modifier surfaces unchanged since v29.0 baseline `1646d5af` per PROJECT.md) + Plan 240-01 GO-02 Admin-column verdicts for the 19-row consumer subset (all 19 rows `NO_INFLUENCE_PATH (rngLocked)` or `NO_INFLUENCE_PATH (semantic-path-gate)`; re-verified at HEAD 7ab515fe).

### Validator actor

Validator (block proposer) has two manipulation surfaces against gameover-state at HEAD:

1. **Block.timestamp drift** — consensus bounds `block.timestamp` drift per-block (typically ±15s under Ethereum mainnet consensus); validator cannot arbitrarily advance time by 120 days to force GOTRIG-240-001 liveness trigger. The 120-day window requires 120 × 86400 = 10,368,000 seconds of cumulative block-timestamp progression; no single validator controls enough consecutive blocks to compound drift meaningfully against this window.
2. **Transaction ordering within a block** — validator can re-order transactions within a single block; but the gameover-trigger predicate `_livenessTriggered()` reads `block.timestamp` and `purchaseStartDay` (both block-committed before tx execution); re-ordering within a block does NOT change the predicate's evaluation for any tx within that block.

Gameover-*trigger* state (GOTRIG-240-001 surface) is therefore not meaningfully manipulable by validator within Phase 240's scope. Gameover-*fulfillment* state — specifically the rngWord produced by VRF or prevrandao-fallback — IS subject to validator 1-bit manipulation via `block.prevrandao` @ AdvanceModule:1322 under the EXC-02 KI envelope (14-day `GAMEOVER_RNG_FALLBACK_DELAY` gate at `:1250`). This is the canonical KI EXC-02 exposure — documented in `KNOWN-ISSUES.md §"Gameover prevrandao fallback"` and forward-cited to Phase 241 EXC-02 per D-19 strict boundary. It is NOT a Phase 240 GO-04 trigger-timing violation — GO-04 scope is strictly the trigger predicate (`_livenessTriggered` + `_handleGameOverPath` safety-escape), NOT the fulfillment rngWord.

**Validator closed verdict: BOUNDED_BY_14DAY_EXC02_FALLBACK** — validator block-delay attacks on the gameover-*trigger* are bounded by (a) consensus-level `block.timestamp` drift limits (cannot advance 120 days), (b) single-threaded-EVM atomicity per Phase 239-03 § Asymmetry B No-Player-Reachable-Mutation-Path Proof (cross-cited; re-verified at HEAD 7ab515fe — single-threaded EVM argument holds structurally), and (c) `_livenessTriggered()` being a pure view function over state already committed before validator-controlled block. Validator block-delay attacks on the gameover-*fulfillment* (rngWord) are bounded by the 14-day `GAMEOVER_RNG_FALLBACK_DELAY` constant at `AdvanceModule:109` which routes withheld VRF fulfillment to the KI EXC-02 prevrandao-fallback surface (accepted exposure). **See Phase 241 EXC-02** for KI acceptance re-verification per D-19 strict boundary. Cross-cites Phase 238 FREEZE-PROOF 19-row Gameover-Flow Validator-column verdicts (7 PATH_BLOCKED_BY_GATE (rngLocked) + 8 EXCEPTION (KI: "Gameover prevrandao fallback") + 4 NO_REACHABLE_PATH; re-verified at HEAD 7ab515fe) and `audit/v30-PERMISSIONLESS-SWEEP.md` 62-row sweep (distribution 24 respects-rngLocked / 0 respects-equivalent-isolation / 38 proven-orthogonal / 0 CANDIDATE_FINDING; re-verified at HEAD 7ab515fe).

### VRF-oracle actor

VRF-oracle (Chainlink) withholding of `rawFulfillRandomWords` for ≥14 days routes to the prevrandao-fallback branch at `_gameOverEntropy:1248-1274`, which calls `_getHistoricalRngFallback:1308` and produces `keccak256(abi.encode(combined, currentDay, block.prevrandao))` at `:1322`. This is the gameover-*fulfillment* path — NOT the gameover-*trigger* path.

Critically: VRF-oracle CANNOT force gameover-trigger activation. Gameover trigger requires `_livenessTriggered()` at `AdvanceModule:530` to return true (120-day Level-1+ path or 365-day Level-0 deploy-idle path). VRF-oracle withholding does not advance `block.timestamp - purchaseStartDay`; VRF-oracle cannot write to `purchaseStartDay`; VRF-oracle cannot execute `_endPhase:634` (the single `phaseTransitionActive = true` site with sole caller `advanceGame:460`). The 14-day fallback path becomes available only AFTER `_livenessTriggered()` has already triggered AND the VRF request has been pending for 14+ days.

On the rngWord-bias side: Plan 240-01 GO-02's 8 prevrandao-fallback rows (GO-240-008..015) carry verdict `EXCEPTION (KI: EXC-02)` with Validator-column `EXCEPTION (KI: EXC-02)` (block-proposer 1-bit prevrandao bias) and VRF-oracle-column `PATH_BLOCKED_BY_GATE (semantic-path-gate)` per Phase 238 FREEZE-PROOF. VRF-oracle's delay-to-fallback is the accepted escape hatch — NOT a gameover-trigger violation.

**VRF-oracle closed verdict: EXC-02_FALLBACK_ACCEPTED** — VRF-oracle withholding ≥14 days routes to the accepted KI EXC-02 prevrandao-fallback surface (`_getHistoricalRngFallback:1301-1325` at HEAD 7ab515fe) — gameover-*fulfillment* path only, NOT gameover-*trigger* path. GO-04 scope is trigger-timing; fulfillment is out-of-scope per D-19 strict boundary (Phase 241 EXC-02 owns acceptance re-verification). **See Phase 241 EXC-02**. Cross-cites Plan 240-01 GO-02 prevrandao-fallback 8 rows + `KNOWN-ISSUES.md §"Gameover prevrandao fallback"` + Phase 238 FREEZE-PROOF 8-row EXC-02 KI-Exception Subset (all re-verified at HEAD 7ab515fe — 14-day `GAMEOVER_RNG_FALLBACK_DELAY` constant at `:109` unchanged; `_getHistoricalRngFallback` body at `:1301-1325` unchanged; `:1322` prevrandao-mix unchanged).

**Narrative attestation (D-13):** All 3 bold-labeled closed verdict tokens appear in this section exactly as written above: `**Admin closed verdict: NO_DIRECT_TRIGGER_SURFACE**`, `**Validator closed verdict: BOUNDED_BY_14DAY_EXC02_FALLBACK**`, `**VRF-oracle closed verdict: EXC-02_FALLBACK_ACCEPTED**` — grep-anchored for 240-02-SUMMARY attestation.

Intersection cardinality 0 over 31 pairwise-distinct (file:line) storage-slot tuples. **Sub-proof verdict: DISJOINT** per D-15.

**Combined Containment Verdict per D-15: BOTH_DISJOINT** — F-29-04 scope is structurally disjoint from VRF-available gameover-jackpot-input determinism at both the inventory level AND the state-variable level. F-29-04 does NOT leak into jackpot-input determinism on the VRF-available branch.

### Set-Equality Proof

By direct enumeration of the 11 pairwise-distinct `INV-237-NNN` IDs across Sets A and B:

- A = {024, 045, 053, 054} (4 elements)
- B = {052, 072, 077, 078, 079, 080, 081} (7 elements)

Membership scan against Set B for every element of Set A:

- `INV-237-024` ∈ A. Is 024 ∈ B? B contains {052, 072, 077, 078, 079, 080, 081} — 024 is NOT in B. ∴ 024 ∉ A ∩ B.
- `INV-237-045` ∈ A. Is 045 ∈ B? 045 is NOT in B. ∴ 045 ∉ A ∩ B.
- `INV-237-053` ∈ A. Is 053 ∈ B? 053 is NOT in B. ∴ 053 ∉ A ∩ B.
- `INV-237-054` ∈ A. Is 054 ∈ B? 054 is NOT in B. ∴ 054 ∉ A ∩ B.

Therefore `A ∩ B = ∅` (intersection cardinality = 0). |A ∪ B| = |A| + |B| − |A ∩ B| = 4 + 7 − 0 = 11 pairwise-distinct `INV-237-NNN` IDs, confirming the complete disjointness.

This set-intersection is a MECHANICAL CHECK over 11 distinct numeric Row IDs — independently reproducible by any reviewer via `comm -12 <(echo -e "024\n045\n053\n054") <(echo -e "052\n072\n077\n078\n079\n080\n081")` producing empty output.

| F29-SLOT-02 | `ticketsFullyProcessed` (bool) @ contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:304 | Read-slot-drained flag; `_swapTicketSlot` resets to false on buffer swap so the new read slot begins undrained. | SSTORE @ Storage:762 (inside `_swapTicketSlot`); SSTORE @ AdvanceModule.sol:218 (`advanceGame` post-drain true); SSTORE @ AdvanceModule:611 (`_handleGameOverPath` post-dual-drain true) |
| F29-SLOT-03 | `ticketQueue[wk]` (`mapping(uint24 => address[])`) @ contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:456 — the "write slot" half-view under the active `ticketWriteSlot` toggle | Per-level ticket buyer queue with `wk = _tqWriteKey(lvl)`; writes during active purchase phase land in the write-slot half; post-swap, the former write-slot becomes the read-slot for drain. `_swapTicketSlot` reverts if the read slot (`rk = _tqReadKey(purchaseLevel)`) is not fully drained (`ticketQueue[rk].length != 0` revert @ Storage:760). | Zero-length gate SLOAD @ Storage:760; append SSTOREs @ Storage:578, 610, 664 (write-key path inside `_queueTickets`); drain SLOADs during `_handleGameOverPath` dual-round at AdvanceModule:587-609 |
| F29-SLOT-04 | `ticketsOwedPacked[wk][buyer]` (`mapping(uint24 => mapping(address => uint40))`) @ contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:460 — write-slot half-view | Per-level-per-address packed (owed << 8 \| rem) ticket-owed state keyed on the same wk as `ticketQueue[wk]`. | F29-SLOT-05 | `ticketCursor` (uint32) @ contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:467 | Ticket-drain progress pointer within the active read-slot `ticketQueue[rk]`. Reset on level-transition; advances monotonically during drain. | SSTORE on level-transition inside `_endPhase` + `_handleGameOverPath`; SLOAD during `_processFutureTicketBatch` iteration |
| F29-SLOT-06 | `ticketLevel` (uint24) @ contracts/storage/DegenerusGameStorage.sol:470 | Current drain-target level (distinct from `level` which is the purchase-level); advances as drain completes each level's read-slot queue. | SSTORE on drain progression (advances with `ticketCursor`); SLOAD during `_processFutureTicketBatch` key computation |

Set C (F-29-04 write-buffer-swap storage slots) = `{F29-SLOT-01, F29-SLOT-02, F29-SLOT-03, F29-SLOT-04, F29-SLOT-05, F29-SLOT-06}` — the 6 distinct storage-slot file:line tuples that the F-29-04 substitution surface touches. Note on `TICKET_SLOT_BIT` and `TICKET_FAR_FUTURE_BIT` constants (@:187, :195): these are `internal constant` — compile-time constants, not storage slots — they parameterize the `_tqWriteKey` / `_tqReadKey` / `_tqFarFutureKey` key-computation functions but do not themselves occupy state. ### Note on GOVAR-240-022/-023/-024 and Set C membership

Plan 240-02's GOVAR-240-022 (`ticketQueue[lvl]` @ Storage:456) = F29-SLOT-03; GOVAR-240-023 (`ticketsOwedPacked[lvl][addr]` @ Storage:460) = F29-SLOT-04; GOVAR-240-024 (composite ticket pointer packing: `ticketCursor` @:467 + `ticketLevel` @:470 + `ticketWriteSlot` @:320 + `ticketsFullyProcessed` @:304) = F29-SLOT-01 ∪ F29-SLOT-02 ∪ F29-SLOT-05 ∪ F29-SLOT-06. In other words, the 3 EXC-03 GOVAR rows from Plan 240-02 are a per-variable (semantic-grouping) VIEW over the same 6 primitive slots enumerated in Set C above. Plan 240-02 correctly flags all 3 with `EXCEPTION (KI: EXC-03)` — these slots do not carry `FROZEN_AT_REQUEST` or `FROZEN_BY_GATE` status for the gameover consumer set (by design: the F-29-04 substitution is precisely the admission that mid-cycle write-buffer ticket contents at the gameover instant are fed into the jackpot via `ticketQueue[rk]` drain at `_handleGameOverPath:587-609`). GO-05's state-variable-level disjointness claim is therefore the stronger statement: the JACKPOT-INPUT slots (Set D, 25 rows excluding EXC-03 labels) are disjoint from the F-29-04 WRITE-BUFFER-SWAP slots (Set C, 6 primitive slots). The substitution surface is contained within GOVAR-240-022/-023/-024; it does NOT leak into the Set D jackpot-input universe.

### Set-Equality Proof

By direct inspection of storage-slot (file:line) identity, for every primitive slot in Set C, test membership in Set D:

- **F29-SLOT-01** = `ticketWriteSlot` @ Storage:320. Does any GOVAR row in Set D declare file:line `Storage:320`? Scan of the 25-row jackpot-input table above: no GOVAR row in Set D cites Storage:320. ∴ F29-SLOT-01 ∉ Set D.
- **F29-SLOT-02** = `ticketsFullyProcessed` @ Storage:304. Scan of Set D: no GOVAR row cites Storage:304. ∴ F29-SLOT-02 ∉ Set D.
- **F29-SLOT-03** = `ticketQueue[wk]` @ Storage:456. Scan of Set D: no GOVAR row cites Storage:456 (the only row in the full 28-row GOVAR universe that cites Storage:456 is GOVAR-240-022, which is excluded from Set D by construction as an EXC-03 member). ∴ F29-SLOT-03 ∉ Set D.
- **F29-SLOT-04** = `ticketsOwedPacked[wk][buyer]` @ Storage:460. Scan of Set D: no GOVAR row cites Storage:460 (only GOVAR-240-023 does, excluded). ∴ F29-SLOT-04 ∉ Set D.
- **F29-SLOT-05** = `ticketCursor` @ Storage:467. Scan of Set D: no GOVAR row cites Storage:467 (only GOVAR-240-024 composite does, excluded). ∴ F29-SLOT-05 ∉ Set D.
- **F29-SLOT-06** = `ticketLevel` @ Storage:470. Scan of Set D: no GOVAR row cites Storage:470 (only GOVAR-240-024 composite does, excluded). ∴ F29-SLOT-06 ∉ Set D.

All 6 Set-C slots are independently absent from Set D. Therefore `C ∩ D = ∅` (intersection cardinality = 0) at per-primitive-slot granularity. |C ∪ D| = |C| + |D| − |C ∩ D| = 6 + 25 − 0 = 31 pairwise-distinct (file:line) storage-slot tuples, confirming the complete disjointness.

This set-intersection is a MECHANICAL CHECK over 31 distinct (file:line) storage-slot tuples — independently reproducible by any reviewer via grep cross-check (sorting Set C and Set D file:line tuples and running `comm -12` produces empty output).

Structural corroboration with `re-verified at HEAD 7ab515fe` notes:

- **Phase 238-02 FWD-01 storage-read set** for the 19-row gameover-flow subset (`audit/v30-238-02-FWD.md` §"PREFIX-GAMEOVER" L94-117 + §"F-29-04 bespoke tails" L522-541) — re-verified at HEAD `7ab515fe`: the VRF-available gameover-entropy subset's FWD-01 storage reads intersect with the jackpot-input pool/winner-state slots (GOVAR-240-001..021, -025..028) but NOT with the F-29-04 write-buffer slots (Storage:304, :320, :456, :460, :467, :470). The 4 F-29-04 bespoke tails (INV-237-024, -045, -053, -054) have their own FWD-01 storage-read enumeration touching the 6 F29-SLOT primitive slots.
- **Phase 239-03 § Asymmetry B phase-transition-gate origin proof** — re-verified at HEAD `7ab515fe`: the phase-transition-gate envelope (used by GOVAR-240-006, -008, -011, -025 in Set D) is structurally distinct from the semantic-path-gate + F-29-04 envelope (used by EXC-03 GOVAR-240-022/-023/-024 = Set C). A Set-D GOVAR gated by phase-transition-gate does not thereby overlap with a Set-C slot gated by the F-29-04 substitution semantics — the two envelopes apply to non-overlapping slot sets.

### Derivation Rule

- State-Variable-Level Verdict: **DISJOINT** (Set C ∩ Set D = ∅; |C|=6, |D|=25, |C∪D|=31)

### Combined Verdict

**BOTH_DISJOINT** — F-29-04 scope is structurally disjoint from VRF-available gameover-jackpot-input determinism at both the inventory level (disjoint `INV-237-NNN` sets) AND the state-variable level (disjoint (file:line) storage-slot tuples between the 6 F-29-04 write-buffer-swap primitives and the 25-slot jackpot-input sub-universe). F-29-04 does NOT leak into jackpot-input determinism on the VRF-available branch.
